key_insight: BiMU keeps Bayesian binary neural networks plastic and uncertain over long non-stationary streams by using a bounded-memory variational update with uncertainty-aware step sizing.
engineering_relevance: This enables always-on edge learning systems to adapt online, query labels only when needed, and maintain reliable out-of-distribution detection under tight compute and memory budgets.
method: Combines the data likelihood with controlled relaxation toward the prior to balance stability, plasticity, and forgetting.
Uses an uncertainty-dependent step size to prevent posterior saturation and preserve epistemic uncertainty.
Supports fully online, buffer-free active querying via Monte Carlo disagreement to reduce label and backpropagation updates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong results on 1000-task Permuted-MNIST and OpenLORIS-Object, including up to 32× savings in labels/updates at matched accuracy.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful control of the bounded-memory variational update and may be sensitive to imbalance, compression, and the quality of uncertainty estimates.
paper_title: Active Continual Learning with Metaplastic Binary Bayesian Neural Networks
